results.label: Confident — GDPR hiring AI, EU
results.input: We are building an AI tool that screens job applicants and ranks them by suitability. We operate in Germany and France.

domains.reasoning: Applicant screening necessarily processes personal data (privacy) and explicitly uses AI to rank candidates (ai).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated operations in Germany and France, both EU member states.

data_types.reasoning: Applicant screening inherently processes behavioural data such as work history, qualifications, and performance signals.

sector.reasoning: Explicitly described as a job applicant screening and ranking tool.

results.label: Confident — US health app
results.input: Our iOS app lets patients in the United States track chronic conditions, log medications, and share data with their doctors.

domains.reasoning: The app processes personal health data (chronic conditions, medications) and transmits it via network to doctors, requiring both privacy and cybersecurity controls.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Users are explicitly described as patients in the United States.

data_types.reasoning: Chronic condition tracking and medication logging are inherently health data; sharing with doctors confirms medical context.

sector.reasoning: An app enabling patients to track conditions and communicate with doctors is a healthcare delivery tool.

results.label: Confident — UK fintech credit scoring
results.input: We provide algorithmic credit scoring to UK banks. Our model uses transaction history and behavioural signals to approve or decline loan applications.

domains.reasoning: Credit scoring necessarily processes personal data (privacy domain) and explicitly uses an algorithmic model (ai domain).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Service is explicitly stated to be provided to UK banks.

data_types.reasoning: Transaction history is inherently financial data; behavioural signals are explicitly stated as model inputs.

sector.reasoning: Credit scoring and loan approval are core financial services.

results.label: Confident — children's education platform, US
results.input: We run an online learning platform for K-12 students in the United States. We collect names, grades, usage data, and video recordings of tutoring sessions.

domains.reasoning: The platform collects and processes personal data (names, grades, usage data, video recordings) from minors.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated as serving K-12 students in the United States.

data_types.reasoning: K-12 students are minors (children); usage data and tutoring session recordings constitute behavioural and communications data.

sector.reasoning: An online learning platform serving K-12 students is explicitly an education sector deployment.

results.label: Confident — EU smart city surveillance
results.input: Our computer vision platform processes CCTV footage across public spaces in Amsterdam and Brussels to monitor crowd density and detect incidents in real time.

domains.reasoning: CCTV footage is personal data (privacy), computer vision is AI/ML, and real-time monitoring systems have network/API exposure (cyber).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated deployment in Amsterdam (Netherlands) and Brussels (Belgium), both EU jurisdictions.

data_types.reasoning: CCTV footage captures individuals' locations and movements in public spaces, which constitute location and behavioural data.

sector.reasoning: Public space monitoring and incident detection are typically municipal/government functions, though private security operators are also possible; inferred from use case rather than explicit statement.

sector.reasoning: Delivery robots operating on public streets are a transport/logistics service.
